title: frangula hintonii
intro: Frangula hintonii, also known as the Pueblo or Mexican buckthorn, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Rhamnaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its laxative and purgative effects, attributed to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of Frangula hintonii include relief from constipation, digestive issues, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Frangula hintonii can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or as a tincture, often used to support digestive health and promote regular bowel movements.
